channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Lawsuit claims Girl Scout cookies contain 'heavy metals,' pesticides
item.description: A new federal lawsuit claims Girl Scout Cookies contain heavy metals, but the organization assures the cookies are safe.
item.dc:creator: Aubree B. Jennings, KTLA 5

item.title: ICE runs out of space, money to detain migrants, report says
item.description: A high-ranking official with Immigration and Customs Enforcement says the agency has run out of money to detain and house more migrants for the time being, according to Reuters.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, KTLA 5

item.title: A government program made tax filing free and more efficient. Musk and DOGE may get rid of it anyway
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Mia Francis, a 22-year-old barista from Boston, filed her taxes on her own this year for the first time, using a free government tax filing program that made it easy because it did most of the work for her.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: FEMA launches review of migrant shelter aid, suggesting smuggling laws were violated
item.description: McALLEN, Texas (AP) — The Trump administration has launched a review of organizations that provide temporary housing and other aid to migrants, suggesting they may have violated a law used to prosecute smugglers.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Columbia grad student's detention will stretch on as lawyers spar over Trump's plan to deport him
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Mahmoud Khalil will remain detained in Louisiana until at least next week but can finally speak to lawyers while they fight the Trump administration’s plans to deport the Columbia University graduate student for his role in campus protests against Israel, a judge decided at a hearing
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ and LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: From soup cans to airplanes, steel and aluminum are a fundamental part of American life
item.description: Steel and aluminum are ubiquitous in Americans' lives. A stainless steel refrigerator holds aluminum soda cans. A stainless steel drum tumbles inside an aluminum washing machine. They're the metals used in cars and airplanes, phones and frying pans, skyscrapers and zippers.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN and ANNE D'INNOCENZIO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: EPA signals it could narrow Clean Water Act protections
item.description: The law requires the EPA to protect so-called “waters of the United States” but there has been significant political back-and-forth.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, NewsNation

item.title: Indiana man accused of beating, killing 5-year-old after abuse
item.description: Court documents paint a harrowing picture of continuous alleged abuse of the boy by Brice Bainum, 32.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, NewsNation

item.title: California Gov Newsom sets Menendez brothers parole board hearing date in bid for clemency
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom announced on his podcast Tuesday that Erik and Lyle Menendez will have a parole board hearing on June 13 as they push for clemency.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: T-Mobile customers to get payments up to $25K next month after data breach: Here's who qualifies
item.description: The $350 million T-Mobile agreed to pay in a 2022 class action settlement will finally start going out to customers next month.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KRON4

item.title: Migrant gang member suspected of murder, sexually assaulting girlfriend is arrested
item.description: A Venezuelan illegal migrant and suspected Tren de Aragua gang member was arrested in Chicago for a fourth time Tuesday after first being ordered deported under the Biden administration in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Passenger attacked flight attendant, swallowed rosary beads on plane to Miami: court docs
item.description: His sister told the FBI agent that they were traveling to Haiti “to flee religious attacks of a spiritual nature.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Police reconnect father and daughter who was kidnapped decades ago
item.description: Police are announcing they've re-connected a father and his daughter, who was kidnapped from New Haven decades ago.
item.dc:creator: Brittany Taylor, NewsNation

item.title: LA woman reunited with cat presumed lost in devastating Palisades Fire
item.description: A California woman who lost her home in the devastating firestorm that engulfed the Palisades experienced a tearful reunion over the weekend with her beloved cat, lost in the charred remains of the neighborhood for some two months.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, WGN 9

item.title: How tech is reducing lines at Clippers games
item.description: Lines are pretty much part of the live event experience - whether it’s getting in, getting food, or grabbing a drink. But tech is trying to change that. Recently, I visited the Intuit Dome, the new home of the Clippers.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5

item.title: Chicago O'Hare Airport shooting leaves 1 injured after fight erupts inside terminal
item.description: A fight inside a terminal at Chicago O'Hare International Airport early this morning led to a shooting outside that left one person injured, reports say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump is dismantling the Education Department. Here's what it does
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump has said he wants his new education chief, Linda McMahon, to “put herself out of a job.” Since Trump took office, staffing at the Education Department has been cut in half, and he has been mulling an executive order to close the agency.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA and COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Study shows rain-soaking atmospheric rivers are getting bigger, wetter and more frequent
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As extreme weather events have hit the world hard in recent years, one meteorology term — atmospheric rivers — has made the leap from scientific circles to common language, particularly in places that have been hit by them. That stands to reason.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Education secretary: Mass layoffs first step toward total shutdown
item.description: Education Secretary Linda McMahon affirmed late Tuesday that mass layoffs are the first step toward shuttering the Education Department — a long-time goal of President Donald Trump's since his time on the campaign trail.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: EU retaliates as Trump steel, aluminum tariffs take effect
item.description: After announcing the response, the EU released a 99-page list of possible items that could be subject to tariffs, including fruit and vegetables, meat, alcoholic beverages, nicotine vapes and chewing gum.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: The US is short nearly 4 million homes
item.description: Builders faced barriers like zoning and supply costs, which make producing affordable homes challenging.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, ABC 4

item.title: Most AAPI adults don't support cutting agencies and want a focus on costs: AP-NORC/AAPI Data poll
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Most Asian Americans, Native Hawaiians and Pacific Islanders do not agree with the strategy of putting entire federal agencies on the chopping block, nor are they broadly on board with mass layoffs of federal workers, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: TERRY TANG and LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Columbia anti-Israel protest ringleader Mahmoud Khalil faces court hearing on detention
item.description: A Manhattan federal judge will hear a challenge to the detention of Mahmoud Khalil, one of the ringleaders of anti-Israel protests at Columbia University last year who the Trump administration is trying to deport.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The EU retaliates against Trump's trade moves and slaps tariffs on produce from Republican states
item.description: The European Union on Wednesday announced retaliatory trade action with new duties on U.S. industrial and farm products, responding within hours to the Trump administration's increase in tariffs on all steel and aluminum imports to 25%.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK and DAVID McHUGH Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Schools use AI to monitor kids, hoping to prevent violence. Our investigation found security risks
item.description: One student asked a search engine, “Why does my boyfriend hit me?” Another threatened suicide in an email to an unrequited love. A gay teen opened up in an online diary about struggles with homophobic parents, writing they just wanted to be themselves.
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE BRYAN of The Seattle Times and SHARON LURYE of The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Court stays execution of Texas man days before he was set to die by lethal injection
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — A Texas appeals court on Tuesday halted the execution of a man who has spent more than 30 years on death row and had been set to die by lethal injection this week over the killings of six girls and young women found buried in the desert near El Paso.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Justice Department is expected to slash public corruption unit, AP sources say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Prosecutors in the Justice Department section that handles public corruption cases have been told the unit will be significantly reduced in size, and its cases will be transferred to U.S. attorney's offices around the country, two people familiar with the matter said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump halts doubling of tariffs on Canadian metals after Ontario suspends electricity price hikes
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump 's threat Tuesday to double his planned tariffs on steel and aluminum from 25% to 50% for Canada led the provincial government of Ontario to suspend its planned surcharges on electricity sold to the United States.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, ROB GILLIES and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: AG concludes charges in gang-related Nevada prison brawl that left 3 inmates dead
item.description: Twenty people have been charged in connection to a 2024 Nevada prison brawl that ended with three dead inmates, authorities said on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: University of Pittsburgh student missing in Dominican Republic: What we know
item.description: The search involves joint efforts by authorities in the Dominican Republic, the United States and India.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, WGN 9

item.title: Police release more details on murder of InfoWars reporter Jamie White as search for suspects continues
item.description: Police in Austin, Texas, believe InfoWars reporter Jamie White was shot and killed when he interrupted suspects possibly burglarizing his car at his apartment complex.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republicans warn Trump not to let trade war drag as he doubles down on tariff threats
item.description: Senate Republicans are warning President Trump to not let his trade war drag on as his latest escalation sent Wall Street further into turmoil.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, ABC 4

item.title: Department of Education lays off nearly half of workforce
item.description: The Department of Education announced Tuesday it was firing nearly half of its workforce, the latest Trump administration move to shrink the federal government that could face swift legal challenge.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Medical chopper reported 'flight control problem' before crash that killed 3 on board in Mississippi
item.description: CANTON, Miss. (AP) — A medical helicopter that crashed in a wooded area in Mississippi reported “a flight control problem” shortly before a Monday crash that killed all three people on board, federal authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US added to human rights watchlist over Trump moves
item.description: The United States has been added to CIVICUS's monitor list of potential human rights threats due to President Trump's restrictive executive orders and institutional cutbacks, which are creating an atmosphere to chill democratic dissent.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, WGN 9
